rationale: X explicitly surfaces the two GT concerns most reviewers cared about (the trust_remote_code default flip, and the deploy-override-vs-auto-detected-pipeline precedence issue, noting the regression test that fixes it), giving it better recall against the ground truth. Y is more thorough and technically deep (functools.cache poisoning, missing try/except around load_deploy_config, lost warning message) with sharper file:line actionability, but entirely misses the single most-discussed GT issue (endpoint restrictions resolved from the wrong pipeline) and contains one factually wrong claim (the 'dead hf_config=None' assignment is actually read on the exception path). Both avoid a labeled LATENT GAP CHECK section.
